sections.title: A: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
sections.passage: Many students dream (1) ...... studying at a prestigious university abroad. It can be a life-changing experience for those who (2) ...... the determination and drive to settle (3) ...... in an unfamiliar country. The challenge of adapting to a new academic culture is the feeling you get when you move somewhere so different from where you (4) ...... up that you are not sure how to behave. You can often be (5) ...... aback by how different the teaching style is in another country. Social customs and unwritten rules can vary enormously, and that can sometimes make it difficult to (6) ...... on with other students and to (7) ...... genuine friendships. They might not approve (8) ...... your study habits or might object (9) ...... ideas you express in class. You might even be banned (10) ...... using certain websites in some countries where internet access is restricted. (11) ...... you were to study in a country such as China, for example, you might find that some platforms are completely blocked. There, students can be forced (12) ...... use a VPN just to read international news. In time, though, most people who study abroad (13) ...... in love with their adopted country and come to appreciate its unique qualities. It does (14) ...... real courage to make such a significant leap, but many graduates agree (15) ...... the experience transforms you forever.

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: My (FRIEND) ...... with Carlos goes back to primary school and is still as strong as ever.
Many teachers complain about their students' (OBEY) ......, but teenagers have always tested boundaries.
Stop being so insecure about other people's success – you're just (JEALOUS) ...... of them.
The whole audience watched (NERVE) ...... as the young violinist stepped onto the stage alone.
We need to ensure that (ABLE) ...... visitors can access every area of the new museum.
My neighbour is incredibly (ARGUE) ...... and seems to enjoy picking fights with everyone on the street.
Hasn't anyone ever told you that it's very (POLITE) ...... to use your phone during a meal?

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
I promised my mum that I would be home before midnight. **made**

My parents ...... despite the fact that it was a school night.

The new law was rejected by everyone who heard about it. **approval**
The new law didn't ...... when it was introduced.

I was feeling tired, so I changed the subject to avoid any conflict. **mood**
I wasn't ...... an argument, so I changed the subject.

Tina felt sad because she found it impossible to connect with anyone at summer camp. **inability**
Tina was depressed because ...... friends at summer camp.

'Would you mind opening the door for me?' Simon said to me. **me**
Simon ...... the door for him.

Don't forget to look after your little brother while I'm at the shops. **care**
Make sure you ...... your little brother while I'm out.
If I ...... the competition, I'd donate half the prize money to a local charity.
'Did you argue with your boss at the meeting?' 'If you had been in that room, you ...... the same.'
If you run into Jessica at the party, ...... her to give me a call when she's free.

'Have you got any news for Sophie?' 'Tell her I'll message her tomorrow if you ...... her.'
The documentary covers some of the most ...... crimes committed in the last century.
Politicians need to understand what life is like for ...... people, not just the wealthy.
Alex tells me he really ...... himself at your housewarming party last weekend.
I know we fell out over that misunderstanding, but I'd love it if we could .......

The lights dimmed, the ...... fell silent, and the performance began.
